org.apache.flink.table.connector.source

## Interface DynamicTableSource

  * All Known Subinterfaces:
    [LookupTableSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/source/LookupTableSource.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.source"), [ScanTableSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/source/ScanTableSource.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.source")

All Known Implementing Classes:
    [HBaseDynamicTableSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/hbase/source/HBaseDynamicTableSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.hbase.source"), [JdbcDynamicTableSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/connector/jdbc/table/JdbcDynamicTableSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.connector.jdbc.table"), [Kafka010DynamicSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/Kafka010DynamicSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table"), [Kafka011DynamicSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/Kafka011DynamicSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table"), [KafkaDynamicSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/KafkaDynamicSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table"), [KafkaDynamicSourceBase](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/streaming/connectors/kafka/table/KafkaDynamicSourceBase.html> "class in org.apache.flink.streaming.connectors.kafka.table"), [SocketDynamicTableSource](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/examples/java/connectors/SocketDynamicTableSource.html> "class in org.apache.flink.table.examples.java.connectors")

* * *




        [@PublicEvolving](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/annotation/PublicEvolving.html> "annotation in org.apache.flink.annotation")
        public interface DynamicTableSource

Source of a dynamic table from an external storage system.

Dynamic tables are the core concept of Flink's Table & SQL API for processing both bounded and unbounded data in a unified fashion. By definition, a dynamic table can change over time.

When reading a dynamic table, the content can either be considered as:

    * A changelog (finite or infinite) for which all changes are consumed continuously until the changelog is exhausted. See [`ScanTableSource`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/source/ScanTableSource.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.source") for more information.
    * A continuously changing or very large external table whose content is usually never read entirely but queried for individual values when necessary. See [`LookupTableSource`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/source/LookupTableSource.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.source") for more information.

Note: Both interfaces can be implemented at the same time. The planner decides about their usage depending on the specified query.

Instances of the above mentioned interfaces can be seen as factories that eventually produce concrete runtime implementation for reading the actual data.

Depending on the optionally declared abilities such as [`SupportsComputedColumnPushDown`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/source/abilities/SupportsComputedColumnPushDown.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.source.abilities") or [`SupportsFilterPushDown`](<../../../../../../org/apache/flink/table/connector/source/abilities/SupportsFilterPushDown.html> "interface in org.apache.flink.table.connector.source.abilities"), the planner might apply changes to an instance and thus mutates the produced runtime implementation.
